zh.lifeSummary: 當Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi於1260年出生時，他的父親Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes65歲，母親Alix de Beauvoir51歲。他和Johanna van Fiennes至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1303年享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi于1260年出生时，他的父亲Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes65岁，母亲Alix de Beauvoir51岁。他和Johanna van Fiennes至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1303年享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Landes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: patronymic from   Lande  .
French: variant of   Lande  .
Altered form of Swiss German and South German   Landis  .

variants.name: Lande
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads, so named from either the dative singular or the plural form of land (see Land 3).
German: from the old personal name Lando, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with lant ‘land’ (see Land ).

French: topographic name for someone living on a heath, moor, French lande (from Gaulish landa ‘space, land’), or a habitational name from any of numerous minor places called La Lande from this word. Compare Lalande .

variants.name: Landis
meanings: Swiss German and South German: nickname for a highwayman or for someone who lays waste to the land, Middle High German landoese. The surname of Swiss German origin is also found in France (Alsace), where it is rare. Compare Landes and Lantis .
Americanized form of German   Landers   3. Alternatively, an altered form of   Landers   4. Compare   Landess  .

History: The ancestor of many of the Americans with the surname Landis was Christopher (or Christian) Landis or Landers from Germany who settled in VA (now WV) in the middle of the 18th century and later moved to NC. The original form of his surname is not known; both the surnames Landis and Landers are found in Germany and they are both established among his descendants, as is the (other) Americanized form Landess. — This is also the name of a Swiss Mennonite family, originating from the canton of Zurich, whose members settled, after the expulsion from Switzerland in the 17th century, in the Palatinate, Germany, and in Alsace, France, or immigrated to America. In both Germany and America the change of the name from Landis to Landes occured. In the first half of the 18th century there were several Mennonites named Landis or Landes who immigrated to PA. The town of Landisville in Lancaster County, PA, is named for one of them.

nameMeanings.name: Heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.

nameMeanings.name: Johan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Older Scottish spelling of Joan , which was traditionally pronounced as two syllables.

variants.name: Jens
meanings: Mainly Danish: variant of   Johan .

variants.name: Jöns
meanings: Mainly Danish: variant of   Johan .

variants.name: Jan
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: Jon
meanings: Simplified spelling of   John   or a short form of   Jonathan .  The name is borne by the Canadian singer  Jon Vickers  (  b. 1926 ) and the American actor  Jon Voight  (  b. 1938 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi was born in 1260, his father, Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes, was 65 and his mother, Alix de Beauvoir, was 51. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Johanna van Fiennes. He died in 1303, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi est né en 1260, son père, Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes, avait 65 ans et sa mère, Alix de Beauvoir, avait 51 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Johanna van Fiennes. Il est décédé en 1303, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi 1260 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes 65 und seine Mutter Alix de Beauvoir 51. Er hatte mit Johanna van Fiennes mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1303 im Alter von 43 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi è nato il nel 1260, il suo padre, Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes, aveva 65 anni e il suo madre, Alix de Beauvoir, aveva 51 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Johanna van Fiennes. Morì nel 1303, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoiが1260年に生まれた時，父Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnesは65歳，母Alix de Beauvoirは51歳であった。彼はJohanna van Fiennesとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1303年に43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi(이)가 1260년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes(은)는 65세였고, 그의 어머니 Alix de Beauvoir(은)는 51세였다. 그는 Johanna van Fiennes(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1303년 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi nasceu em 1260, seu pai, Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes, tinha 65 anos e sua mãe, Alix de Beauvoir, tinha 51 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Johanna van Fiennes. Ele faleceu em 1303, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi родился в 1260 г., его отцу по имени Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes было 65 лет, а матери, которую звали Alix de Beauvoir, 51. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Johanna van Fiennes. Он умер в 1303 г. в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heer Johan van Landes Heer van  Sainghin-en- Mélantoi nació en 1260, su papá, Écuyer Gérard II d'Landes-Esnes, tenía 65 años y su mamá, Alix de Beauvoir, tenía 51 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Johanna van Fiennes. Murió en 1303, a la edad de 43 años.
